title: pypodman is not able to work on rhel7.5 python3.4.9
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:
**kind bug**


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
With the environment of rhel7.5 and python 3.4.9, pypodman raises a TypeError while I'm trying to get the remote machine's containers info. I also tried this environment not for the remote feature, like `pypodman images` or `pypodman ps`, this error still exists.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
##### On remote machine:
```
# hostnamectl                                         
   Static hostname: localhost.localdomain
Transient hostname: dhcp-2-7.nay.redhat.com
         Icon name: computer-laptop
           Chassis: laptop
        Machine ID: cd196ab74fbf40448d31037ceafbcf05
           Boot ID: 7811c3c51c0d46ad90c327e8153dcbcd
  Operating System: Fedora 27 (Workstation Edition)
       CPE OS Name: cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:27
            Kernel: Linux 4.18.7-100.fc27.x86_64
```
```
# podman version
Version:       0.10.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.9.7
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```
```
# podman info
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-1.10.3-1.gite558bd5.fc27.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.10.3, commit: 27d18947e07ae525daacba333e67a98a6296fc5d'                   
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "27"
  MemFree: 174825472
  MemTotal: 11980427264
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-36.gitad0f525.fc27.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc5+dev
      spec: 1.0.0
  SwapFree: 5162135552
  SwapTotal: 6043987968
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: dhcp-2-7.nay.redhat.com
  kernel: 4.18.7-100.fc27.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 99h 28m 45.01s (Approximately 4.12 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

1. build podman from [upstream](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/tutorials/podman_tutorial.md#building-and-installing-podman)

2. `systemctl enable io.podman.socket`

3. `systemctl start io.podman.socket`

4. get socket status:
```
systemctl status io.podman.socket
● io.podman.socket - Podman Remote API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/io.podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
   Active: active (listening) since Fri 2018-10-12 17:00:47 CST; 2 days ago
     Docs: man:podman-varlink(1)
   Listen: /run/podman/io.podman (Stream)
```

5. `podman varlink -t 5000000 unix:/run/podman/io.podman`

##### On host machine:
```
# hostnamectl
Static hostname: localhost.localdomain
Transient hostname: dell-per630-03.rhts.eng.pek2.redhat.com                                              
         Icon name: computer-server
           Chassis: server
        Machine ID: 132a8e31ef294cd4ba9b6b771a2953ff
           Boot ID: 12654aac39b94bd89fb7a573286fc9ec
  Operating System: CloudForms
       CPE OS Name: cpe:/o:redhat:enterprise_linux:7.5:GA:server                                         
            Kernel: Linux 3.10.0-862.el7.x86_64
      Architecture: x86-64
```
```
# python3 --version
3.4.9
```

1. [install python package podman from upstream](https://github.com/containers/libpod/tree/master/contrib/python/podman#releases)

2. [install pypodman from upstream](https://github.com/containers/libpod/tree/master/contrib/python/pypodman#releases) 

3. `export PATH="$PATH:/root/.local/bin"`

4. `pypodman -l root --host 10.66.2.7 ps`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@dell-per630-03 pypodman]# pypodman -l root --host 10.66.2.7 ps
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/root/.local/bin/pypodman", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('pypodman==0.0.0', 'console_scripts', 'pypodman')()
  File "/root/.local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pypodman-0.0.0-py3.4.egg/pypodman/main.py", line 35, in main
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.4/genericpath.py", line 19, in exists
    os.stat(path)
TypeError: argument should be string, bytes or integer, not PosixPath
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Be able to have the same result as `podman ps` on the remote machine.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Always happen on this eviroment.


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical
